title: Audit OpenCode skill/plugin format against current layout
description:
Compare OpenCode's skill, command, agent, plugin, and hook contracts against this repo's current Claude Code / Codex layout. Output: a written gap analysis posted to the parent feature issue or this issue's notes that subsequent tasks can plan against.

## What to investigate
- Skill discovery paths OpenCode actually loads (`.opencode/skills/`, `~/.config/opencode/skills/`, `.claude/skills/`, `.agents/skills/`) vs our `skills/<name>/SKILL.md`
- Whether OpenCode strict-validates SKILL.md frontmatter and tolerates Claude Code-only fields (`allowed-tools`, `model`, `effort`)
- Whether `/skill-name` invocations should map to OpenCode skills or commands (`.opencode/commands/<name>.md`)
- Agent format differences for `agents/deps.md` (`mode`, `permission`, `tools`)
- Plugin module shape (JS/TS exporting hook functions vs our `hooks/prompt-skill-suggest.mjs` UserPromptSubmit hook)
- Distribution: `opencode.json` `plugin` field, npm-distributable plugin packages, marketplace conventions

## Deliverable
A short gap-analysis writeup that lists for each surface (skills, commands, agents, hooks, manifest) what works as-is, what needs adaptation, and what should be scoped out.
acceptance_criteria: Gap analysis covers skills, commands, agents, hooks, and distribution; each gap is mapped to a follow-up issue (existing or to be created).
notes:
# OpenCode Compatibility Gap Analysis

Confirmed against https://opencode.ai/docs/{skills,commands,agents,plugins,config} on 2026-04-25.

## Key finding: tolerance, not strict validation
OpenCode SKILL.md parsers **ignore unknown frontmatter fields** ("Unknown frontmatter fields are ignored" — skills doc). Our Claude Code-only fields (`allowed-tools`, `model`, `effort`) will be silently ignored by OpenCode rather than rejected. This collapses the "frontmatter compat" question to: do OUR `name` and `description` fields match OpenCode's required shape? Yes (kebab-case names, length-bounded descriptions).

## Surface-by-surface

### Skills — works with discovery fix
- OpenCode loads SKILL.md from: `.opencode/skills/<name>/`, `~/.config/opencode/skills/`, `.claude/skills/<name>/`, `.agents/skills/<name>/`
- Our layout is `skills/<name>/SKILL.md` at repo root — **not** discovered by OpenCode out of the box
- Required fields: `name` (kebab-case, 1-64 chars), `description` (1-1024 chars). All current skills already comply.
- Optional OpenCode fields: `license`, `compatibility` (e.g. `compatibility: opencode`), `metadata` (string-string map)
- **Recommendation**: add `.opencode/skills/`, `.claude/skills/`, and `.agents/skills/` symlinks pointing at `../skills/` (or per-skill symlinks). Single source of truth stays in `skills/`. → drives skills-qnj

### Commands vs skills (decision)
- OpenCode commands (`.opencode/commands/<name>.md`) are template-driven prompts with frontmatter `template`, `agent`, `subtask`, `model` — different model
- Our skills are invoked as `/tartinerlabs:<name>` in Claude Code; in OpenCode they would be invoked as skills, not commands
- **Decision**: distribute as OpenCode skills (Claude-compatible path), not commands. No command files needed.

### Hooks — port required
- `hooks/prompt-skill-suggest.mjs` is a Claude Code UserPromptSubmit hook reading stdin, emitting JSON via `additionalContext`
- OpenCode plugins are JS/TS modules exporting plugin functions that return hook objects (different lifecycle, different API surface)
- **Recommendation**: implement as a small in-repo plugin under `.opencode/plugins/` reusing the keyword-match logic. Acceptable to scope out for v1 if effort is too high. → drives skills-cl0

### Agents — port or scope out
- `agents/deps.md` is a Claude Code subagent
- OpenCode agents (`.opencode/agents/<name>.md`) use frontmatter `description` (req), `mode` (primary/subagent/all), `model`, `temperature`, `permission`
- **Recommendation**: mirror to `.opencode/agents/deps.md` with translated frontmatter. Low priority. → drives skills-9cg

### Distribution / install — manual clone path
- `opencode.json` `plugin` array accepts npm packages, but skills/agents/commands are filesystem-only — no remote-repo skill install via config
- No `opencode skill install <repo>` CLI documented
- **Recommendation**: document a clone-and-symlink (or git-submodule) install path in README. Optionally publish a thin npm plugin that auto-registers our skills via filesystem read at startup, for the convenience case. → drives skills-2e4

### CI validation
- Existing `Skills` workflow validates skills.sh + Context7
- OpenCode validation can be a YAML-schema check (frontmatter `name` + `description` shape) plus a discovery-path existence check on the symlinks
- No public OpenCode CLI validator to call directly → drives skills-iws

## Net assessment
Compatibility is mostly cheap: discovery-path symlinks unlock 90% of value because OpenCode tolerates extra frontmatter. Hook port is the only meaningful net-new code.

title: Add OpenCode distribution metadata and install docs
description:
Add the manifest/config files OpenCode needs to recognise this repo as a plugin source, and document install paths in README.md and CONTRIBUTING.md alongside Claude Code, Codex, skills.sh, and Context7.

## Work
- Add `opencode.json` (or `.opencode/` directory) at repo root if needed for plugin/skill registration
- README install snippet for OpenCode users (project-level and global)
- CONTRIBUTING note on how skill changes flow to OpenCode users
- Keep `package.json.version` as the single shared source of truth (per CLAUDE.md convention)
acceptance_criteria: README.md has an OpenCode install section; CONTRIBUTING.md documents how skills reach OpenCode users; manifest files (if any) are committed and validated.

title: Make skills discoverable by OpenCode and validate frontmatter
description:
Expose `skills/<name>/SKILL.md` to OpenCode's loader and ensure frontmatter parses under OpenCode's schema without dropping Claude Code-specific behaviour.

## Approach options (decide during this task)
- Symlink or mirror `skills/` into a path OpenCode discovers (`.opencode/skills/`, `.claude/skills/`, or `.agents/skills/`)
- Or change our skills root to a path OpenCode loads natively, keeping back-compat for Claude Code / Codex / skills.sh
- Confirm OpenCode tolerates the extra frontmatter keys (`allowed-tools`, `model`, `effort`); if it strict-validates, decide whether to namespace under `metadata` or keep them and document the divergence

## References
- OpenCode skills doc: https://opencode.ai/docs/skills/
- Claude-compatible path: `.claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md` (per OpenCode docs)
- Required frontmatter: `name` (lowercase-kebab, 1–64 chars), `description` (1–1024 chars)
acceptance_criteria: A fresh OpenCode workspace can list and invoke at least one skill from this repo without manual file copying; SKILL.md frontmatter validates under OpenCode without warnings; Claude Code / Codex distribution unaffected.
notes:
Resolution: added `.opencode/skills` directory symlink → `../skills/` (git mode 120000). When this repo is opened as a project root by OpenCode, all 14 skills are discovered via OpenCode's native `.opencode/skills/<name>/SKILL.md` path. Single source of truth remains `skills/`; Claude Code, Codex, skills.sh, and Context7 distribution unchanged. Verified all 14 SKILL.md files have OpenCode-compliant required frontmatter (`name` kebab-case, `description` <1024 chars). Extra Claude Code fields (`allowed-tools`, `model`, `effort`) ignored by OpenCode per spec — no changes needed.

Skipped: per-skill `.claude/skills/` and `.agents/skills/` symlinks. Single `.opencode/skills/` is sufficient for the discovery acceptance criterion. If users need other paths for global install, that's docs work in skills-2e4.

title: Make plugin compatible with OpenCode
description:
Ship the tartinerlabs skill set so it can be consumed by OpenCode (https://opencode.ai) alongside the existing Claude Code, Codex, skills.sh, and Context7 distribution channels.

## Why
OpenCode is an open-source AI coding agent that supports SKILL.md-based agent skills (incl. `.claude/skills/` and `.agents/skills/` discovery paths), markdown commands, markdown agents, and JS/TS plugins. Adopters who use OpenCode currently have no first-class way to install our skill bundle. Most of our SKILL.md content should already be reusable; the gaps are discovery paths, frontmatter schema, hook wiring, agent format, and distribution metadata.

## Scope
- Skills discoverable from an OpenCode-recognised path
- Frontmatter validates against OpenCode's required schema (`name`, `description`)
- Hook (`prompt-skill-suggest.mjs`) ported or scoped out
- Agent file (`agents/deps.md`) adapted or scoped out
- Distribution metadata + README/CONTRIBUTING install instructions
- CI validation extended

## Out of scope
- Rewriting skill content
- Replacing Claude Code or Codex packaging
acceptance_criteria: OpenCode users can install or load tartinerlabs skills using a documented path; skill frontmatter validates under OpenCode's schema; existing Claude Code / Codex / skills.sh / Context7 distribution remains unchanged; CI validates the OpenCode path.

title: Extend Skills CI workflow to validate OpenCode distribution
description:
The existing `Skills` CI workflow validates skills.sh and Context7 distribution on push to main. Extend it (or add a sibling job) to validate that the OpenCode-facing surface stays healthy: frontmatter parses, discovery path resolves, and any plugin module typechecks.

## Work
- Add a CI step that runs OpenCode's skill validator (if available as a CLI) or a custom YAML/JSON schema check
- Wire the step into `.github/workflows/` alongside existing skills.sh + Context7 jobs
- Pin third-party actions to commit hashes per repo convention
acceptance_criteria: CI fails if a skill's frontmatter regresses against OpenCode's schema or if the discovery path breaks; existing skills.sh + Context7 jobs are unchanged.

title: Adapt agents/deps.md for OpenCode agent format
description:
`agents/deps.md` is a Claude Code subagent. OpenCode markdown agents live in `.opencode/agents/<name>.md` with frontmatter `description` (required), `mode` (primary/subagent/all), `model`, `temperature`, `permission`.

## Work
- Decide whether to mirror `agents/deps.md` into an OpenCode-discoverable path or scope agents out for v1
- If adapting: map Claude Code subagent fields to OpenCode's frontmatter, keeping the original file for Claude Code

## Reference
- https://opencode.ai/docs/agents/
acceptance_criteria: Either agents/deps.md is consumable from OpenCode in subagent mode, or agents are documented as Claude Code-only for v1 with a follow-up issue if relevant.

title: Port prompt-skill-suggest hook to OpenCode plugin module
description:
`hooks/prompt-skill-suggest.mjs` is a Claude Code UserPromptSubmit hook that surfaces relevant skills via `additionalContext`. OpenCode plugins are JS/TS modules that export plugin functions returning hook objects (different shape, different lifecycle).

## Work
- Identify the equivalent OpenCode plugin event (likely the prompt/turn-start event)
- Wrap the existing keyword-match logic in an OpenCode plugin module
- Decide where the plugin is published (in-repo `.opencode/plugins/`, separate npm package, or skipped for v1)
- Document config snippet for users (`opencode.json` `plugin` array)

## Reference
- https://opencode.ai/docs/plugins/
acceptance_criteria: OpenCode users can opt into the same skill-suggestion experience, OR the hook is explicitly scoped out for v1 with a note in CONTRIBUTING.
